title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They believe that the house was built by a famous architect.
questions.explanation: Tests complex passive with perfect infinitive.
questions.secondSentence: The house (1) _________ by a famous architect.
solutions: is thought to have been built

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'll definitely help you with the chores,’ said my roommate.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with infinitive structures.
solutions: promised to give me a hand

questions.firstSentence: I only managed to solve the problem because you gave me a hint.
questions.explanation: Tests the conditional structure 'If it hadn't been for'.
questions.secondSentence: If it (1) _________ your hint, I wouldn't have solved the problem.

questions.firstSentence: I am sure they didn't see us because it was so dark.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative deduction in the past.
questions.secondSentence: They (1) _________ us because it was so dark.

questions.firstSentence: We had hardly started the picnic when it began to pour with rain.
questions.explanation: Tests inversion with the restrictive adverb 'Hardly'.
questions.secondSentence: Hardly (1) _________ the picnic when it began to pour with rain.

questions.firstSentence: It is much easier to speak English than to write it.
questions.explanation: Tests comparative structures with 'not as... as'.
